abstract:
Imbalanced learning represents one of the most enduring and formidable challenges in data mining and machine learning. Despite decades of continuous research, the ability to learn effectively from data characterized by skewed class distributions remains a critical bottleneck, often limiting the practical utility of both traditional machine learning and modern deep learning models in real-world scenarios. Recent breakthroughs have significantly deepened our conceptual understanding of these issues while simultaneously uncovering complex new hurdles. 

Given the rapid evolution of the field, this paper provides a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art strategies designed to mitigate class imbalance. Moving beyond typical surveys that focus almost exclusively on classification, we expand our scope to include emerging techniques in imbalanced regression and the nuances of deep long-tail learning. Furthermore, we map the landscape of practical applications, spanning a diverse range of disciplines from management science to industrial engineering. Finally, we identify and discuss nascent challenges and future research directions essential for advancing the frontier of imbalanced learning.
